queries.sql:
WITH
  1024 AS size,
  3000 AS n_points,
  6 AS turns
SELECT
  toInt32(round(size / 2 + (4 + (number / n_points) * (size / 2 - 8)) * cos(turns * 2 * pi() * number / n_points))) AS x,
  toInt32(round(size / 2 + (4 + (number / n_points) * (size / 2 - 8)) * sin(turns * 2 * pi() * number / n_points))) AS y,
  toUInt8(round(128 + 127 * sin(turns * 2 * pi() * number / n_points))) AS r,
  toUInt8(round(128 + 127 * sin(turns * 2 * pi() * number / n_points + 2.094))) AS g,
  toUInt8(round(128 + 127 * sin(turns * 2 * pi() * number / n_points + 4.188))) AS b
FROM numbers(n_points)
FORMAT PNG
SETTINGS output_format_image_width = 1024, output_format_image_height = 1024

spec.name: Image: rainbow spiral (1024×1024)
spec.description: Plots 3000 points along a 6-turn polar spiral as an RGB FORMAT PNG image, hue-cycling colour by phase. Needs ClickHouse 26.6+ for FORMAT PNG. Columns must be named x,y (integer) and r,g,b (UInt8) per the PNG format contract; canvas size comes from output_format_image_width/height (default 1024).

cfg.alt: A rainbow-coloured spiral traced by 3000 points

queries.sql:
SELECT
  toUInt8(number % 256) AS r,
  toUInt8(intDiv(number, 256) % 256) AS g,
  toUInt8(128 + 64 * sin(number / 4096.0)) AS b
FROM numbers(65536)
FORMAT PNG
SETTINGS output_format_image_width = 256, output_format_image_height = 256

spec.name: Image: RGB gradient (256×256)
spec.description: A cheap 256×256 RGB FORMAT PNG with no explicit x,y columns — row order alone (row-major, left-to-right then top-to-bottom) fills the canvas. Needs ClickHouse 26.6+ for FORMAT PNG; canvas size set via output_format_image_width/height SETTINGS.

queries.sql:
WITH 256 AS size
SELECT
  toUInt8(60) AS r,
  toUInt8(160) AS g,
  toUInt8(240) AS b,
  toUInt8(255 * exp(-1 * (
    pow((toFloat64(number % size) - size / 2) / (size / 4), 2) +
    pow((toFloat64(intDiv(number, size)) - size / 2) / (size / 4), 2)
  ))) AS a
FROM numbers(size * size)
FORMAT PNG
SETTINGS output_format_image_width = 256, output_format_image_height = 256

spec.name: Image: transparent RGBA glow (256×256)
spec.description: A soft blue radial glow with a Gaussian alpha falloff — an r,g,b,a FORMAT PNG demonstrating the panel's checkerboard background option for transparent images. Needs ClickHouse 26.6+ for FORMAT PNG.

cfg.alt: A soft blue glow fading to full transparency at the edges, shown over a checkerboard background

queries.sql:
-- Inspired by ClickHouse's SQL ray-tracing demos (github.com/ClickHouse/RayTracer);
-- this is an original, minimal analytic ray/sphere scene, not a copy of that project.
WITH
  256 AS size,
  0.0 AS cx, 0.0 AS cy, -3.0 AS cz, 1.0 AS cr,
  -2.0 AS lx, 3.0 AS ly, -1.0 AS lz
SELECT
  x, y,
  toUInt8(greatest(0, least(255, round(255 * (0.05 + 0.55 * shade))))) AS r,
  toUInt8(greatest(0, least(255, round(255 * (0.08 + 0.55 * shade))))) AS g,
  toUInt8(greatest(0, least(255, round(255 * (0.35 + 0.60 * shade))))) AS b
FROM (
  SELECT
    x, y, hit,
    if(hit, greatest(0.0, nx * lux + ny * luy + nz * luz), 1.0 - v * 0.5) AS shade
  FROM (
    SELECT
      x, y, hit,
      (px - cx) / cr AS nx, (py - cy) / cr AS ny, (pz - cz) / cr AS nz,
      (lx - px) / llen AS lux, (ly - py) / llen AS luy, (lz - pz) / llen AS luz,
      v
    FROM (
      SELECT
        x, y, u, v, dx, dy, dz, t, hit,
        t * dx AS px, t * dy AS py, t * dz AS pz,
        sqrt(pow(lx - t * dx, 2) + pow(ly - t * dy, 2) + pow(lz - t * dz, 2)) AS llen
      FROM (
        SELECT
          x, y, u, v, dx, dy, dz,
          (-b - sqrt(greatest(disc, 0))) / 2 AS t,
          disc >= 0 AS hit
        FROM (
          SELECT
            x, y, u, v, dx, dy, dz,
            2 * (-cx * dx + -cy * dy + -cz * dz) AS b,
            (cx * cx + cy * cy + cz * cz - cr * cr) AS c,
            2 * (-cx * dx + -cy * dy + -cz * dz) * 2 * (-cx * dx + -cy * dy + -cz * dz)
              - 4 * (cx * cx + cy * cy + cz * cz - cr * cr) AS disc
          FROM (
            SELECT
              x, y, u, v,
              u / len AS dx, v / len AS dy, -1.0 / len AS dz
            FROM (
              SELECT
                x, y, u, v, sqrt(u * u + v * v + 1.0) AS len
              FROM (
                SELECT
                  toInt32(number % size) AS x,
                  toInt32(intDiv(number, size)) AS y,
                  (toFloat64(number % size) / size - 0.5) * 2 AS u,
                  (0.5 - toFloat64(intDiv(number, size)) / size) * 2 AS v
                FROM numbers(size * size)
              )
            )
          )
        )
      )
    )
  )
)
ORDER BY y, x
FORMAT PNG
SETTINGS output_format_image_width = 256, output_format_image_height = 256

spec.name: Image: ray-traced sphere (256×256)
spec.description: An original, minimal analytic ray/sphere-intersection scene with Lambertian shading over a sky-gradient background, rendered entirely in SQL as an RGB FORMAT PNG. Inspired by ClickHouse's SQL ray-tracing demos (github.com/ClickHouse/RayTracer) — not a copy of that project's code. CPU cost grows with the pixel count (size×size rows); keep size small (256 here) unless you deliberately want a slower, higher-resolution render — a 1024 render can take noticeably longer. Needs ClickHouse 26.6+ for FORMAT PNG.

cfg.alt: A shaded sphere lit from one side, rendered by ray-tracing math in SQL, over a gradient sky background
